medications.indication_rationale: CONTRAINDICATED in suspected acute aortic dissection - aspirin and anticoagulants worsen bleeding from the aortic tear. Although aspirin is given for suspected MI, it must be withheld here; the priority is immediate emergency transfer.
contraindications_in_this_case: Do NOT give aspirin or any blood thinner - worsens haemorrhage from the dissection

medications.indication_rationale: Rate and blood-pressure control (e.g. intravenous beta-blockade to a target heart rate/BP) reduces aortic wall shear stress in dissection, but is delivered in the emergency/hospital setting with monitoring - not over telehealth.
contraindications_in_this_case: Administer only in a monitored in-person/emergency setting; not a telehealth medication

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Self-transport / taking aspirin / waiting to see if the pain settles
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Type A dissection mortality rises rapidly per hour; self-transport delays care, aspirin worsens the bleed, and waiting risks fatal rupture. Immediate ambulance is essential.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stay completely still and flat and be moved only by paramedics; breathe slowly and calmly to limit blood-pressure surges.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Minimising exertion and blood-pressure spikes reduces shear stress on the tearing aorta while awaiting transfer.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not take aspirin or any blood thinner.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Antiplatelet/anticoagulant therapy worsens haemorrhage from an aortic dissection.

patient_education_points.point: This sounds like a possible tear in the main artery from your heart (aortic dissection), which your Marfan syndrome makes more likely - it is a life-threatening emergency, not a simple heart attack or muscle strain.
Call 000 for an ambulance right now - do not drive yourself. Stay completely still and flat, breathe slowly, and do NOT take aspirin or any blood-thinning tablet.
If you become unresponsive, your roommate should call 000 again and start CPR immediately.
The ambulance and hospital will control your blood pressure and do an urgent scan; you are likely to need emergency surgery to repair the tear.

follow_up_plan.interval: Immediate emergency ambulance transfer to a cardiothoracic centre; emergency surgery anticipated
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: CT aortography findings and dissection type/extent, blood-pressure/heart-rate control, surgical repair, and long-term Marfan aortic surveillance, beta-blocker adherence and family screening post-recovery
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Loss of consciousness while waiting - the roommate should start CPR and keep 000 on the line.

must_include_items: Recognition of probable acute aortic dissection (not MI or musculoskeletal pain) in known Marfan syndrome
Immediate 000/ambulance with pre-notification of a cardiothoracic centre - no self-transport
Keep the patient still and flat with calm breathing to limit BP surges
acceptable_alternatives: 'Tear in the main artery', 'aortic dissection', 'life-threatening blood-vessel emergency' are acceptable framings
Immediate ambulance is required; no lower-tier alternative is acceptable
errors_of_commission: Recommending aspirin (as for MI) - a dangerous, harmful error
Allowing self-transport / advising to wait and see
Reassuring it is likely a muscle strain
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL EMERGENCY: A pass requires (1) recognising probable aortic dissection and calling an immediate ambulance AND (2) explicitly withholding aspirin. Recommending aspirin, allowing self-transport/delay, or anchoring on a benign cause is a dangerous, automatic-fail output risking fatal aortic rupture.
